title: COM Operator Alphabet v1.1
description: The 13 operators of the COM Grammar. Each operator carries a function definition, examples, and a typical risk-weight range. Risk weights are engineering defaults, not ontological constants.

operators.name: Fixation
operators.function: Lock the current state into a confirmed, registered, externally-visible form. Stop the leak. Stabilize the node before any forward movement.
examples: Formulate a decision in explicit terms
operators.key_meaning: The node stops hanging in unformalized state.

operators.name: Inversion
operators.function: Reverse the direction of the current route. What was pursued becomes what is avoided. Not negation of fact — change of operational reading.
examples: From 'they owe me' to 'what do I actually do next'
operators.key_meaning: Not denial of facts; change of operational reading.

operators.name: Break
operators.function: Forced termination of a line that does not convert to a working state. Hard stop, not pause.
examples: Terminate the cycle of explanations without result

operators.name: Hold
operators.function: Temporary withdrawal of attention, energy, or decisions from the node. Managed conservation, not avoidance.
operators.key_meaning: Not flight; managed preservation of optionality.

operators.name: Pump
operators.function: Inject additional resource into the node. Used only when the node is viable and reinforcement makes sense.
operators.key_meaning: Reinforce only viable nodes; do not pump dead-ends.

operators.name: Transfer
operators.function: Redistribute load, responsibility, deadline, or function. The node does not vanish; it stops hanging on the previous carrier.
examples: Move responsibility to the other party
operators.key_meaning: The node persists, but no longer hangs on the previous carrier.

operators.name: Closure
operators.function: Full completion of the node with no residual tail. Differs from Fixation: Fixation formalizes; Closure removes the tail.
operators.key_meaning: No leftover tail or unfinished pull.

operators.name: Boundary
operators.function: Establish a frame of admissibility. Define what is allowed, when, by whom.
examples: 'This is not my zone of responsibility'
operators.key_meaning: A frame of admissibility, not a wall.

operators.name: Cleanup
operators.function: Remove noise, residue, old hooks, false meanings attached to the node.
examples: Drop a sense of debt where there is no actual debt
Clear shame or fear loading from a node
operators.key_meaning: Remove load that is not structurally part of the node.

operators.name: Reframe
operators.function: Change the class label of a node without changing the facts.
operators.key_meaning: Change of class, not change of fact.

operators.name: Compression
operators.function: Reduce an overloaded node to its minimal working form.
examples: A long drama into a single decision
A global fear into a concrete next step
operators.key_meaning: From wide drama to narrow operator.

operators.name: Separation
operators.function: Split nodes that have been incorrectly fused.
operators.key_meaning: Untangle bound nodes that should be addressed independently.

operators.name: Return
operators.function: Bring an externalized function back into the self-model or base contour.
examples: Return the right to evaluate to oneself
Return self-regulation instead of monitoring the other
Remove dependence of self-verification on an external node

telemetry_states.name: Clean
telemetry_states.description: Budget free, no overheating. Nominal or expansion mode permitted.

telemetry_states.name: Loaded
telemetry_states.description: Background tension. Only medium-risk operators advised.

telemetry_states.name: Overheating
telemetry_states.description: High risk of breach. High-risk operators forbidden.

telemetry_states.name: Breach
telemetry_states.description: Breach in progress. Active chains must be stopped or moved into Recovery.

budget_gates.name: Allowed
budget_gates.description: All operator classes admissible up to AllowedTotalRisk.

budget_gates.name: Hold-Only
budget_gates.description: Only Hold and low-risk stabilizers permitted.

budget_gates.name: Recovery-Only
budget_gates.description: Budget Recovery active; only Fixation, Hold, Cleanup permitted.

risk_tolerance_factors.note: These are engineering defaults. They are calibration targets, not universal constants. No threshold may be used as proof of the model.
